title: Sand mining feud: BJP neta burnt alive in car, 2 more killed in midnight ambush in Chhatisgarh
content: A violent sand mining turf war in Chhattisgarh's Koriya district resulted in a triple murder.  A BJP functionary was burnt alive in a luxury SUV, with two others dying from injuries after a suspected planned ambush. Police are investigating the escalating tensions between rival groups involved in the sand business.

title: Sooryavanshi row: BCCI secretary breaks silence on Sri Lanka incident
content: BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia clarified the board's stance on the on-field altercation involving Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Vishen Halambage. Saikia emphasised that disciplinary actions are solely the purview of match officials, not the BCCI. Sri Lanka Cricket has reportedly sanctioned Halambage for provocative remarks and Dickwella for excessive appealing following the Super Over match.

title: 'Spied with CCTVs, assaulted for talking to neighbours': Doctor husband held after newlywed's death
content: A 26-year-old newlywed woman in Ambernath died by suicide, with her family accusing her doctor husband and in-laws of dowry harassment and physical abuse. The deceased's family alleges constant surveillance via CCTV cameras and demands for more gifts. The husband, mother-in-law, and brother-in-law have been arrested following a police complaint.

title: Investor Micheal Burry, who calls Nvidia overvalued says: No, thank you to SpaceX stock
content: Michael Burry, known for predicting the 2008 housing crash, has no stake in SpaceX, deeming its valuation excessive and put options too costly. He also reiterated concerns about Nvidia's overvaluation and questioned Anthropic's nearly $1 trillion valuation, comparing AI stock rallies to the dot-com bubble's final stages.

title: 'Jumped to save mother': Indian teen dies in New York after falling from horse carriage
content: A tragic accident in New York's Central Park claimed the life of 18-year-old Indian student Romanch Mahajan. While on a horse-drawn carriage ride, the vehicle bolted, and Romanch heroically jumped to save his mother, suffering a fatal head injury. The incident has reignited calls to ban horse-drawn carriages in the park.

title: Captain Shubman Gill joins Kohli, Tendulkar in elite club after 154 vs Afghanistan
content: Shubman Gill's stellar 154 against Afghanistan in Lucknow secured a commanding 170-run victory for India, clinching the ODI series. This remarkable innings marked Gill as the seventh Indian captain to score over 150, joining legends like Tendulkar and Kohli. His performance underscores his growing stature in Indian cricket.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: India eyes $2.5 billion loans from World Bank, ADB as subsidy costs rise
content: India is in discussions with the World Bank and Asian Development Bank for approximately $2.5 billion in loans to support urban infrastructure and job creation. This move aims to bolster capital for government spending, which has been impacted by rising oil prices and increased subsidy expenditures due to the Middle East conflict.

title: 'Not Bhagwant Mann': AAP cites '1,191 different angles' to defend Punjab CM in 'sacrilege' row
content: Punjab finance minister and AAP leader Harpal Singh Cheema asserted that a video allegedly showing chief minister Bhagwant Mann in a sacrilegious act is fake. Forensic reports from two independent laboratories confirm the individual in the video is not Mann, suggesting a defamation attempt by the opposition. This comes after the Akal Takht issued an edict against Mann over the clip.

title: What Accenture informed employees worldwide in its memo
content: Accenture has altered its salary hike structure for the June cycle, impacting over 780,000 employees globally. Half of approved raises will be a one-time June payout, with the other half added to base pay. This aims to provide immediate cash while managing payroll costs. Promotion increases remain solely in base pay.

title: Govt considers durability certificates for buildings after unsafe construction concerns
content: The Centre is considering a 'durability certificate' for buildings due to rising concerns over unsafe construction, following court-ordered demolitions of unfit structures. This initiative aims to improve construction quality and safety, especially after recent incidents like a building collapse in Delhi and a dilapidated structure's partial collapse in Varanasi.

title: Watch: Young fan teaches Messi a new celebration after historic WC hat-trick
content: Lionel Messi ignited the FIFA World Cup 2026 with a historic hat-trick against Algeria, becoming the oldest player to achieve the feat and equalling Miroslav Klose's record. A heartwarming viral video captured Messi learning a new goal celebration from a young fan, sparking anticipation for his potential record-breaking strike.

title: ‘Batwara 1947’ teaser out: Sunny Deol revisits the pain of partition
content: The teaser of ‘Batwara 1947’ has finally been unveiled, offering a glimpse into the emotional turmoil of India’s Partition. Led by Sunny Deol, the film explores courage and humanity amid chaos, while Preity Zinta appears briefly. Directed by Rajkumar Santoshi, the historical drama releases worldwide on August 14, 2026.

title: Harry Kane and Kate Goodland turned a childhood romance Into marriage
content: Harry Kane enters his third World Cup as England's captain and all-time top scorer, fresh off a 36-goal Bundesliga season with Bayern Munich. Away from the pitch, his wife Katie Goodland has been part of his story since childhood. The pair met at age four in Chingford, started dating as teenagers in 2011, and married in 2019. A qualified fitness coach with a Sports Science degree, Goodland now balances her own career with raising their four children while supporting Kane through England's latest push to end six decades of World Cup hurt.

title: Crissy Froyd claims Mike Silver liked old bikini photos after Russini Story Row
content: Crissy Froyd has accused The Athletic's Mike Silver of liking old bikini photos on her Instagram immediately after she published a column about the Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini scandal, alleging the behaviour was targeted. Froyd, who was previously fired from USA Today Sports over her criticism of Russini, has alleged that at least six female reporters admitted to her that they traded intimacy for NFL access. Meanwhile, Russini has resigned from The Athletic, which is now conducting an internal investigation.

title: New claim about Jorge Messi’s health reframes Lionel Messi’s World Cup Tears
content: Lionel Messi revealed that his emotional reaction after Argentina’s World Cup win was unrelated to football. According to comments made by journalist Eduardo Feinmann on Radio Mitre, concerns about the health of Messi’s father, Jorge Messi, may have been behind the tears. Despite the personal situation, Messi starred in Argentina’s 3-0 victory over Algeria.

title: Sold cigarettes above printed price: Manufacturer, retailer fined Rs 10 lakh
content: A consumer commission in Aligarh has fined a cigarette manufacturer and a retailer Rs 10 lakh for overcharging a customer Rs 20 above the printed price. The commission ruled the seller acted as the manufacturer's agent, making both liable for unfair trade practices. The duo must also compensate the complainant.

title: 'Forced to drink urine, gave electric shocks': Pune woman alleges 15 yrs of abuse by 'guru'
content: Pune police arrested a self-styled guru and seven associates for allegedly sexually exploiting and cheating a woman. The guru, Radhamohan Mishra, reportedly subjected the victim to severe abuse for 15 years, including physical and sexual assault, and forced her to transfer property. Investigations revealed plans for an escape tunnel and recovered incriminating electronic devices and valuables.

title: Why BCCI is planning early IPL start from 2027? Saikia reveals the reasons
content: The BCCI is planning to kick off the IPL from March 10-May 15 starting in 2027, aiming to escape extreme weather. This shift seeks to mitigate disruptions from heatwaves and pre-monsoon rains affecting late-season matches. The tournament will maintain its 74-match format, with domestic schedules also being reviewed to align with the earlier start.

title: New deal at an old venue: From world wars to US-Iran peace, why Versailles matters
content: In a historic move at France's Palace of Versailles, US President Trump and Iranian President Pezeshkian signed a 14-point MoU to end hostilities. The agreement includes a military ceasefire, nuclear non-development pledge, sanctions relief, and frozen asset release. This landmark deal, aiming for a broader peace, echoes Versailles' legacy of significant international accords.

title: 'Won't be nuked, think of it, Bibi': Trump reveals how he convinced Netanyahu for Iran deal
content: US President Donald Trump declared a new peace deal with Tehran safeguards Israel from a nuclear threat, a claim made at the G7 summit. He asserted the agreement addresses Israel's primary existential concern, despite reports of Israeli opposition and critical media commentary. Trump also voiced criticism of Netanyahu's regional conduct.

title: Can you build a house next to a heritage site? What the law says
content: Constructing in the vicinity of India’s ancient landmarks comes with stringent regulations designed to protect these sites. Development is largely prohibited within 100 meters of designated monuments, preserving their integrity. Beyond this zone, any building plans necessitate official consent to protect heritage values.

title: FIFA WC: Jude Bellingham scripts history, becomes youngest European to ...
content: Jude Bellingham became the youngest European player to feature in four major tournaments, achieving this milestone at 22 years and 353 days. He marked the occasion with a goal in England's 4-2 World Cup opener against Croatia. Bellingham's early career and commitment to the national team were highlighted, with his 50th cap potentially coming against Ghana.

title: German passenger kicks cabin crew on Frankfurt-Hyderabad flight after trolley hits his leg
content: A German flight crew member on board a Lufthansa plane flying from Frankfurt to Hyderabad has lodged a criminal complaint against a fellow traveler. The situation escalated when the passenger, after having his leg accidentally brushed by a trolley, became confrontational and responded with an obscene gesture.

title: 'Because he wants to score': Henry tears into Ronaldo after Portugal's stumble vs Congo
content: Cristiano Ronaldo faced criticism after Portugal's draw against DR Congo, with Thierry Henry highlighting his movement as detrimental. Henry argued Ronaldo's desire to score obstructed teammates like Bruno Fernandes, hindering team opportunities. Despite a stellar international career, Ronaldo's performance lacked impact, with minimal shots and no chances created in the match.

title: Iran's missile war and the growing case for India's Rocket Force
content: India is considering a dedicated Rocket Force, inspired by Iran and Pakistan's missile use. This force would enhance conventional deterrence and deep-strike capabilities. Key systems like Pralay, BrahMos, Pinaka, and the upcoming LR-LACM will form a layered arsenal, offering precision firepower and battlefield dominance against evolving threats.

title: Pakistan's Fatima Sana scripts history, becomes first captain to achieve big WC feat
content: Fatima Sana etched her name in Women's T20 World Cup history with a remarkable all-round performance, scoring an unbeaten 55 and taking 3 wickets. Despite her heroic efforts, Pakistan narrowly lost to South Africa by two wickets in a thrilling contest. Sana's historic feat, however, couldn't secure a victory for her team.

title: Iran-US deal signed: 62 million barrels set to leave Hormuz as Asia braces for oil glut
content: The Strait of Hormuz is set to reopen after a US-Iran deal, potentially flooding Asian markets with millions of barrels of crude. Refiners, already well-supplied and reducing processing, now face worries of oversupply. This shift from earlier fears of shortages is reflected in falling oil prices and market contango.

title: What is the legacy badge? Why Messi and Ronaldo are wearing it at FIFA World Cup
content: Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo are among the first to wear the new FIFA World Cup Legacy badge at the 2026 tournament. This special sleeve patch honors players with exceptional longevity and achievements, recognizing their enduring impact on World Cup history. The badge celebrates their record-equalling sixth appearances and significant contributions to the sport.

title: Trump sanctions waiver on Russian crude expires: What it means for India amid US-Iran peace deal
content: The US Treasury did not issue an extension on Wednesday for its sanctions waiver covering Russian seaborne oil, which expired at midnight. However, neither President Donald Trump nor senior administration officials clarified whether the lapse would result in the sanctions being reinstated.

title: Sena (UBT) split buzz: 5 of 6 Uddhav MPs 'moved out of Delhi' ahead of party parliamentary meeting
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut issued a whip for a mandatory parliamentary meeting, prompting the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena to reportedly move five of its six defecting MPs out of Delhi. Raut warned of action against absentees, asserting the party's readiness to face consequences and vowing to retaliate against attempts to split the party.

title: What Iran and Ukraine reveal about the importance of 'Atmanirbhar' defence push
content: Global conflicts highlight defence self-reliance, with Iran and Ukraine showcasing resilience through domestic production. India, already prioritizing indigenous defence capabilities, leverages a diverse ecosystem of missiles, aircraft, and naval systems. This tailored approach, proven in Operation Sindoor, emphasizes asymmetric warfare and export potential, fostering strategic autonomy and economic growth through a unified national effort.

title: CA final result released: Direct link to download scorecards here
content: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India has released the CA Final May 2026 examination results. Candidates can now check their scorecards online. The results are available on the official ICAI websites. Students need their registration and roll numbers to access their marks. The scorecard details subject-wise marks and the qualifying status.

title: 'She was alive for 2 hrs': Ghaziabad man alleges hospital doors shut on 4-yr-old rape victim
content: A father's plea for accountability echoes after two hospitals allegedly refused to treat his critically injured 4-year-old daughter, who later died. The Supreme Court is hearing the case, questioning the hospitals' actions. The child was found bleeding hours after a brutal assault, with her father asserting timely medical care could have saved her life.

title: Messi's sweet 16: Six World Cups, sixteen goals - A look at Leo's every goal
content: Leo Messi, once criticized for his World Cup record, has now equaled Miroslav Klose's 16-goal tally.  After a disappointing 2018, the 38-year-old is set to extend his lead over Kylian Mbappe. Messi also holds records as the oldest and youngest World Cup goal scorer for Argentina.

title: Ram Mandir donations under scrutiny: RJD MP sends legal notice amid irregularity allegations
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust has received a legal notice from an RJD MP demanding detailed disclosures on Ram Mandir donations and their expenditure. This follows allegations of financial irregularities, which the Trust has denied. Opposition leaders have called for an investigation and questioned the handling of funds.

title: 'I call it deemocracy': Agnimitra Paul's swipe after series of egg attacks on TMC leaders
content: West Bengal witnesses a surge in egg-throwing incidents targeting Trinamool Congress leaders. BJP leader Agnimitra Paul calls this 'deemocracy', citing public anger over alleged corruption and misgovernance. Recent events include attacks on Kunal Ghosh and Soumitra Banerjee. Residents also targeted a vehicle linked to Madan Mitra. These protests highlight growing public dissatisfaction with the ruling party.

title: 'Messi, Messi' chants follow Ronaldo after Portugal's draw against Congo - Watch
content: Cristiano Ronaldo faced a challenging start to his World Cup 2026 journey, failing to score for Portugal in a 1-1 draw. Following the match, Democratic Republic of the Congo fans chanted Lionel Messi's name, a stark contrast to Messi's recent hat-trick. Ronaldo, however, maintained his composure, responding with a smile and applause.

title: Ukrainian drones strike Moscow’s oil refinery again; flames, thick plumes of smoke seen
content: Moscow's oil refinery in Kapotnya was hit by a Ukrainian drone attack on Thursday, causing visible flames and smoke. This incident follows earlier drone strikes on Russian energy infrastructure, contributing to a growing fuel shortage. Meanwhile, Russia launched its own attacks on Kyiv and Sumy. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy discussed war-ending strategies with global leaders.

title: Tamil Nadu Assured Pension Scheme: Government frames rules, procedures
content: The rules provide for a two-stage exit option from the TAPS framework in case eligible government staff wish to exit TAPS

title: Messi event row: Aroop Biswas appears after skipping three police summons
content: Former West Bengal sports Minister Aroop Biswas arrived at the Bidhannagar Police Commissionerate headquarters around 9.55 a.m., after skipping three previous summonses

title: West Bengal moves Supreme Court against early release of 1993 Bowbazar blast convict
content: In a judgment delivered on June 5, the High Court directed Rashid Khan’s premature release, holding that the period of incarceration already undergone was sufficient to meet the objectives of punishment and deterrence

title: T.N. Governor’s address: TVK govt. to establish Investor Promotion Commission to fast-track approvals for companies
content: In his address, the Governor said the government is firm that industries must grow and workers must thrive in order to achieve the target set by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay of transforming Tamil Nadu into a $1.5-trillion economy by 2036

title: Escalating cost of fresh fish upends ordinary household budgets
content: With the seasonal trawling ban in place, most of the daily catch comes from traditional fishing boats, including plank-built catamarans and dinghies fashioned from discarded styrofoam containers, which venture out to sea at dusk and return by dawn

title: Sea Cadet Corps invites applications for 2026 batch
content: Children aged 10 to 12, studying in Classes V to VII, can collect forms at the INS Circars swimming pool on June 20

title: My son will not hold any position in AIADMK, says Edappadi K. Palaniswami
content: “He will remain only a primary member of the party and will not occupy any post in the organisation,” he told journalists after the Governor’s address to the Assembly

title: T.N. CM Vijay announces Kuruvai Special Package for paddy cultivation at a cost of ₹134.83 crore
content: Due to the delay in southwest monsoon, the Mettur dam was not opened on the customary date of June 12; a release states the CM has announced the Kuruvai Special Package to protect farmers’ livelihoods and encourage paddy cultivation

title: Mettur Dam not to be opened for delta irrigation on June 12
content: The government will decide on the release of water for irrigation if the region receives heavy rainfall in the coming days

title: Air Force ferrying NEET question papers by aircraft
content: Bundles flown to more than 20 locations across the country; papers delivered to several northern States, while dry runs were conducted at selected locations across southern India

title: Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan holds  review meeting for NEET re-examination preparations
content: Emphasising on the importance of coordination at all levels, the Minister informed that officers designated by the Ministry will reach all the States to coordinate the activities pertaining to the re-examination process and will report to the Command Centre headed by the DG, NTA

title: T.N. Assembly: Governor Arlekar hails rendering of National Anthem twice, calls it dawn of a new era
content: “It brings immense satisfaction that, after a hiatus of three years, the Governor’s address was read in its entirety within the House without any disruption today,” said Lok Bhavan in a post on X, quoting the Governor

title: Supreme Court defers hearing of plea challenging NEET-UG re-test to July
content: While the re-test is scheduled for June 21, Justice Narasimha’s Bench, before which the matter is to be listed, is scheduled to sit only after July 13, effectively rendering the plea infructuous.

title: Banning Telegram will not stop paper leaks: Kejriwal
content: Arvind Kejriwal asserted that banning the messaging platform was not the solution to the problem and claimed that the authorities were not serious about tackling the issue

title: Bihar govt. allocates 15 acres to TTD for construction of Lord Venkateswara temple
content: Besides the main shrine, the temple complex proposed on a site close to the Ganga near Patna comprises a Kalyana Mandapam, a Pushkarini and other infrastructure to cater to the devotees

title: AMMA general body meeting in Kochi on June 21 likely to be stormy
content: The Malayalam film body’s leadership is unlikely to initiate any disciplinary action against the four members before the general body meeting, according to people close to the development

title: TVK-led government failed to secure Cauvery water for kuruvai cultivation: Edappadi Palaniswami
content: Mr. Palaniswami said although the government had announced 436 schemes across various departments at a Cabinet meeting, they did not find mention in the Governor’s address

title: War heroes, national icons to replace British-era names on 21 roads in Secunderabad Cantonment
content: Bolton Road will become Atal Bihari Vajpayee Road, Noble Road will be renamed A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Road and Lytton Road will bear the name of Netaji Subash Chandra Bose

title: One dead, two injured in fire in Bandra
content: According to Mumbai’s civic body, Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) is confined to electric wiring, electrical installations, household articles, etc., in Room No. 701 and 702 on the 7th floor of a Ground + 7 floored building

title: Dharmasthala case: One phone call being read out of context, says Prakash Raj
content: Actor Prakash Raj remarked his involvement in the case is being read out of context only due to a phone call with Chinna C.N.

title: Telegram CEO Durov’s allegation on Reliance role in access disruption needs evidence
content: To understand the significance of the allegation, it is important to understand how the internet works

title: Nava Kerala Sadas assault case: High Court issues notice to accused on SIT plea against bail
content: The notice is issued in a petition moved by the SIT against the anticipatory bail granted to the accused by the District and Sessions Court, Alappuzha

title: PM Modi in Paris LIVE updates: Modi arrives in Paris, meets members of Indian diaspora
content: Modi is scheduled to attend VivaTech 2026 alongside French President Emmanuel Macron and interact with the Indian community

title: Three killed as lorry rams stranded goods vehicle on NH-163 in Telangana’s Jangaon
content: According to police, the accident was reported around 2 a.m. after the goods carrier which was transporting grocery items from Hyderabad to Devaruppula, suffered a tyre burst near the bridge

title: Telangana’s EAGLE force tracks drug smugglers through Odisha forests, seizes 500 kg marijuana
content: Acting on specific intelligence, a patrol team led by Sub-Inspector of Police K. Ravi Prasad tracked the vehicle for nearly 80 km in the night-long pursuit

title: Explained | The ‘kafir screenshot’ case in Kerala
content: The screenshot came into the public domain on the eve of the general elections in the Vadakara constituency

title: Governor’s address passes off smoothly as first session of 17th T.N. Assembly begins; House to meet for three days
content: The session began with the rendition of the State song Tamil Thaai Vazhthu, followed by the National Anthem. This was a break from House convention where the National Anthem is played only at the end of the Governor’s Address. This time, it was played towards the end, too

title: The best mangoes in South India, according to farmers who grow them
content: We ask farmers across Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Telangana one question: Which mango would you choose above all others? From Tamil Nadu’s Sappattai to Karnataka’s Appemidi, they reveal the fruits they cherish most — and why preserving heirloom mangoes matters

title: Organ trade racket: ED searches leading private hospitals in Kerala
content: Sleuths from the Kochi zonal unit of the ED fanned out to multi-specialty hospitals in Ernakulam, Thiruvananthapuram, Kollam, and Kottayam districts on June 18

title: Governor’s address in T.N. Assembly: Illicit mining and tax evasion will be dealt with an iron hand, says Arlekar
content: The Governor was giving his first address to the State Assembly after the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK)-led coalition government headed by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay assumed office

title: Govt. ratifies 8.25% EPF interest rate for FY'26, to be credited this month
content: Earlier on March 2, the CBT in a meeting chaired by the Union Labour Minister Mansukh Mandaviya had decided to fix 8.25% rate of interest for the financial year 2025-26, marking the third consecutive year this rate has been maintained

title: ‘Operation Tiger’: Six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs skip parliamentary body meeting
content: “They will be issued a show cause notice. Action will be taken as per party discipline,” Anil Desai said

title: Parliament panel moots higher education spending, says NEP’s 6% GDP target unmet
content: The Committee noted that the percentage increase in the Department of Higher Education's budget estimates (BE) for 2025-26 vis-a-vis the BE for 2024-25 was lower than the previous year

title: Calcutta High Court refuses interim order on Ritabrata Banerjee’s appointment as Bengal LoP
content: The court, however, called for affidavits by all parties to examine the larger question of whether the Speaker acted within the ambit of his powers while recognising the LoP.

title: Voting begins for election to two Rajya Sabha seats in Jharkhand
content: Three candidates — BJP-backed Independent nominee Parimal Nathwani, JMM's Baidyanath Ram and Pranav Jha of the Congress — are in the fray for the two seats

title: Odisha approves nearly ₹76,612 crore-worth projects; set to create 50,517 jobs
content: Tata Power Renewable Energy Limited has proposed to establish an ingot and wafers along with a Solar PV Cell Manufacturing Unit in Ganjam with an investment of ₹10,000 crore

title: Result of Karnataka Legislative Council polls to be announced in the evening
content: The ruling Congress, which has 37 members in the 75-member Upper House, is expected to achieve a majority with victory assured in four seats

title: Indian teenager dies in horse carriage accident during his first trip to New York
content: Romanch Mahajan, 18, was on his first trip to New York with his family when the incident took place on Wednesday, June 17, The New York Times reported.

title: Alert issued against Nipah in Kerala from April to September
content: Previous outbreaks and spillover cases suggest that the virus is found to have been active in the State in the the six-month period starting in April; health officials lay focus on Kozhikode, Malappuram, Palakkad, and Thrissur districts

title: Tamil Nadu Governor's address highlights: Rajendra Arlekar says two-language policy will continue in State
content: DMK MLAs, led by Udhayanidhi Stalin, staged a protest outside the Tamil Nadu Assembly, raising slogans against the TVK government and Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay over law-and-order issues

title: KSCA urged to host major cricket matches in Kalaburagi
content: Kushal Patil, coordinator for the Raichur zone, said 13 acres of land had been donated near the Kalaburagi airport for a proposed cricket facility

title: Habbey Teron to be BJP candidate for Assam Assembly Deputy Speaker
content: ''I am pleased to announce that Dr Habbey Teron, Hon'ble MLA from Amri (ST) Hills, will be our candidate for the post of Deputy Speaker of the Assam Legislative Assembly,'' CM Sarma said

title: Successive govts failed to speed up work on irrigation projects in Cauvery and Krishna basins in Karnataka, says senior SC advocate
content: Government of Karnataka needs to double allocation for irrigation projects, according to Mohan Kataraki

title: Andhra Pradesh RTC unions’ JAC opposes private e-buses, lease of depot land; protests on June 23-24
content: The unions want APSRTC to buy and run the electric buses, recruit 10,000 staff and add 4,000 buses, and have called red-ribbon protests on June 23 and 24

title: Seafarers issue of ‘utmost importance’: Modi to Trump
content: PM Modi posted on social media saying the two sides had reviewed U.S.-India cooperation in trade, energy, defence, technology and people-to-people ties

title: Congress jabs govt. over Iran-U.S. MoU signing, calls it severe setback to PM's foreign policy
content: Jairam Ramesh argued that the MoU represents a serious blow to the U.S. that along with Israel started the war with Iran on February 28 this year with maximalist aims that have just not been realised

title: Andhra CM calls for change in crop cultivation strategies to suit El Nino conditions
content: The Chief Minister called for crop diversification, a wider shift to natural farming, and plans to grow pulses in high market demand

title: Andhra Pradesh to remove 43 doctors for long absence from duty: Health Minister
content: The Health Minister said some doctors had stayed away for nearly four years and ignored show-cause notices, in the government’s second such action in a month

title: 2 Arrested In Chandigarh Chemist's Murder After Dramatic Escape Attempt
content: During the probe, investigations traced the movement of the accused, who had fled Chandigarh immediately after commission of the crime.

title: EPFO Withdrawal Via UPI, ATM Likely By Month End, Check Limit
content: Users will be allowed instantly withdraw up to 75 per cent of EPF balance into a bank account using UPI and UPI-enabled ATM access.

title: Raids On Many Hospitals Across Kerala In Organ Trafficking Probe
content: Kerala raids target hospitals and residences linked to organ trafficking racket and forged documents.

title: Caste Survey, Anti-Corruption Drive: Vijay Has His Work Cut Out
content: Tamil Nadu Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar outlined the policy roadmap of the new Vijay-led TVK government.

title: After Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan Chase Cuts Anthropic Access For Hong Kong Staff
content: JPMorgan Chase has stopped its staff in Hong Kong from accessing Anthropic’s AI models, in a sign of intense scrutiny on the technology’s use outside the U.S., the Financial Times

title: "Transparency Essential": Ex-Rajya Sabha MP Questions RSS Funding Sources
content: Senior Congress leader and former Rajya Sabha MP Husain Dalwai called for a comprehensive and independent inquiry into the sources of funds received by the RSS and the manner in which those funds are utilised.

title: Why India Has A Seat At The G7 Table Without Being A Member
content: India is not a formal member of the G7, which includes the US, UK, Canada, France, Germany, Italy and Japan. Yet, it is regularly invited as a special guest, showing how much importance the group now gives to New Delhi's voice.

title: Who Is Noam Shazeer? AI Pioneer Behind Gemini Now Joining OpenAI
content: His exit comes less than two years after Google spent about $2.7 billion in a deal to bring him back to the company.

title: Weather Live Updates: Intermittent Rain, Thunderstorm Across Delhi-NCR Till June 21, Says Weather Office
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain Alert Live Updates: Delhi stays pleasant with temperatures below normal and AQI in satisfactory range

title: "What To Believe?" US Order On AI Models Leaves Anthropic Workers Confused
content: Nearly a week after the surprise order, many workers say they still have little clarity on why the restrictions were imposed.

title: 3/9 MPs At Key Meet: The Six Who Can Break Uddhav Thackeray's Sena, Yet Again
content: Team Uddhav leader Sanjay Raut has alleged that the party MPs in Maharashtra are being offered up to Rs 15 crore each to switch sides and join the Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde-led faction.

title: Facial Recognition On Public Buses? US City Sparks Debate Privacy Debate
content: Images captured by cameras aboard the buses would immediately be checked against any active alerts, generated when a missing person, banned rider or someone on a law enforcement watch list designated by the transit authority is identified.

title: An Ancient Oak Tree Said To Have Sheltered Legendary Robin Hood Has Died
content: The 1,200-year-old Major Oak in Sherwood Forest is believed to have died after it didn't sprout leaves this spring, the Royal Society for the Protection of Birds said.

title: Key Changes In Tax-Saving Bonds For Small Investors Soon? What We Know
content: Reforms to target three key barriers that have limited retail participation in bonds: product availability, market access and operating costs.

title: One Of The World's Most Important Climate Threats Has Image Problem
content: Many dynamic processes shape this ocean current, so we still don't know exactly how fast the circulation will change or even its future trajectory.

title: Why China's New Mega Dam On Brahmaputra Is Worrying India
content: China is building the world's largest hydroelectric dam in the lower parts of the Yarlung Tsangpo River in Tibet, about 50 kilometres from the border with India.

title: Opinion: Ringside View | FIFA World Cup 2026: Magic Of Cristiano Ronaldo Missing — But Why?
content: On a day when Lionel Messi found the net, Kylian Mbappe delivered again and Harry Kane added his name to the scoresheet, Cristiano Ronaldo's was conspicuously absent.
